meaning: n.边,旁边,面,侧面,胁,一方,(一个)方面,摆架子adj.旁边的,侧面的,副的,枝节的vi.站在同一边
examples: It measures 20 yards from side to side.
The ship rolled from side to side.
We sat side by side on two wicker seats.
On the asset side, one of the portfolios is the funded reserve for grants.
The implication is that the skipper bats with more care for his side or the situation.
